title: acmanthera fernandesii
intro: Acmanthera fernandesii, also known as the 'São Tomé vine', is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to the island of São Tomé in the Gulf of Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Consuming Acmanthera fernandesii has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
